output:
**Alex → Engineer** — He holds a Naval Engineering degree and routinely solves complex technical problems on the yacht. He's the clear fit.

**Donnie → Deckhand** — He has years of direct deckhand experience on other ships, plus strong rigging, safety, and maintenance skills. His mechanic background reinforces hands-on problem-solving on deck.

**Emily → Neither role (hospitality/people-facing duties)** — She struggles with technical diagrams (ruling out engineer), and while she can handle basic deckhand tasks, she lacks enthusiasm and explicitly prefers people-oriented work. Assigning her elsewhere also avoids interpersonal friction: her grudge against Alex and irritation with Donnie's hygiene would undermine teamwork in either role.

This allocation also keeps Alex and Donnie — who have strong rapport and proven teamwork under pressure — working in complementary roles, which maximizes operational efficiency.
